codeReview.summary: The feature fixes generic type-parameter emission for declarations and the added regression tests cover the reported Box/Pair/identity paths, but the codegen still emits invalid zero values for generic named types such as Pair[A, B] on Result error paths because go_zero_value drops type arguments. That leaves the implementation short of the milestone requirement that generic structs, functions, and methods emit valid Go type parameter declarations and uses.

issues.description: go_zero_value returns `name + "{}"` for every generic nominal type, so a zero value for `Pair[A, B]` becomes `Pair{}` instead of `Pair[A, B]{}`. Any generated function or method returning `Result<Pair[A, B], str>` (or another generic nominal type on an error path) will still produce invalid Go, so generic type uses are not fixed comprehensively.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, mission contract, and commit 5e39353. The patch correctly repairs generic declarations and receivers and adds meaningful regression coverage, but a remaining blocking bug in generic zero-value emission means the feature does not yet fully satisfy the generic Go codegen requirement.
